Junkrat's Loot Hunt is back again, running from May 8 - 11! Earn lootboxes by completing challenges that unlock as you play!
Our Nintendo Switch 2 version has officially launched! Please be sure to download the correct version for your platform from the Nintendo eShop.

Tags have been added for New Maps and Reworked Maps. Any map with a New or Reworked tag gives bonus Battle Pass XP in Quick Play and an automatic +10 Competitive Points in Competitive Play. The penalty for losing in Competitive Play on a map with one of these tags is also reduced, so give them a shot and don’t worry as much about losing!
A new Random Map option has been added as a 4th choice. The Random Map will never be one of the 3 visible maps.

When a map has 6 or more votes than any other map it wins by a Landslide, skipping the roll entirely. For example, King’s Row with 7 votes does not win by a Landslide against Dorado with 2 votes, because King’s Row only has 5 more votes than Dorado. 8 votes ensures a Landslide in 5v5 and 9 votes in 6v6. Even the Random Map option can win by a Landslide!

New Attack and Defend icons have been added to map cards for Hybrid and Escort, allowing players in Quick Play to know which side they’ll play if this map wins. In Competitive Play these just let players know which side they’ll play first.
At least 2 different game modes will now be offered across the 3 visible maps.
When you vote for a map that loses, this map will be offered to you sooner in future Map Votes.
When no one votes for a map that loses, this map won’t be offered as soon in future Map Votes to anyone in this match.
While voting, it is no longer possible to determine which of your teammates voted for which map.

raw.text: 5 week long event
Each week you can choose your faction, Overwatch or Talon

Once you select your faction, you can complete faction-specific challenges to unlock rewards in the weekly pass. Once you unlock the whole pass, you can either change factions and complete that pass as well (earning those faction-specific rewards) as a Double Agent or choose to stay loyal to your initial faction and complete the Prestige Pass to unlock lootboxes!

interpreted.metric_phrase: weekly pass and Double Agent / Prestige Pass system

raw.text: At the end of each week, completed passes for each faction will be tallied up around the world to establish a global winner for the week - each completed pass will help your faction succeed!
You'll get a special phase reward at the end of each week depending on which faction was victorious that week!

At the end of the 5-week event, each faction's overall progress will be summed up and a global winner will be declared! Look out for an awesome Overwatch or Talon themed Echo skin recolor and a nice in-game surprise to thank you for fighting in the global conflict!

interpreted.notes: Final reward includes Echo skin recolor

raw.text: Rewards you can earn over the course of the event!
75 Base Lootboxes
12 Epic Lootboxes
7 Legendary Lootboxes
9 New Voice Lines
31 Player Icons, Titles, Sprays, and Namecards

interpreted.metric_phrase: Player Icons, Titles, Sprays, and Namecards

raw.text: 2 Weapon Charms
1 of 2 faction-themed Legendary Echo skins
Exclusive Legendary titles (and a secret hidden one!)

interpreted.notes: Reward — includes a hidden secret title

raw.text: Year of the Horse (February 13th through March 2nd) Happy Lunar New Year! We're changing it up a bit this year by bringing back fan-favorite mode, Roadhog's Catch-a-mari, with a New Year twist. Collect adorable Lunar Pachi by eliminating your opponents to earn LNY cosmetics!

interpreted.metric_phrase: Lunar New Year event with Roadhog's Catch-a-mari

raw.text: "Praise" added as a new default function of the Comms Wheel.
Tapping the Comms Wheel button now tells your allies how awesome they are with Praise voice lines that are unique to each hero.

interpreted.metric_phrase: tap behavior triggers Praise voice line

raw.text: By default, the Comms button functionality now mimics the functionality of the Ping Button, meaning there is a very short delay (.18 seconds by default) between pressing the button and the wheel opening.

interpreted.notes: Comms Wheel default behavior now mirrors Ping Wheel

raw.text: This new functionality can be disabled in the Options with the setting "Enable Quick Press Praise". The delay can be modified with the setting "Comms Wheel Press Delay".

interpreted.metric_phrase: new options Enable Quick Press Praise / Comms Wheel Press Delay
interpreted.notes: New options to disable/tune Praise behavior

raw.text: This short delay is immediately canceled when any mouse movement or right stick movement is detected, for players that want to flick to a Comms Wheel option.
Praise can be assigned to the Comms Wheel in the Options, but it is not assigned to the Comms Wheel by default.

interpreted.metric_phrase: Praise can be assigned to wheel

raw.text: Heroes now emit voice lines that suggest setup locations to their team when players use neutral pings during the Setup Phase while defending in Attack/Defend modes.
Heroes now have more specific internal monologue voice lines for when they request healing and no supports are available to help.
Heroes on defense in Escort and Hybrid are now more likely to notice when a payload is being pushed by a sneaky player and call it out with a voice line.

Heroes will now automatically call out to their team with a voice line when they detect an invisible hero nearby (Sombra). This uses the same cooldown and tuning as when heroes detect Widowmaker aiming at them and warn their allies with "sniper" voice lines.

blizzard_commentary: Uses same cooldown/tuning as Widowmaker sniper callout

raw.text: Heroes can aim at an allied Jetpack Cat and ping her to request a pick up at their location with a voice line.

interpreted.metric_phrase: On My Way ETA in chat

raw.text: Thanking a support for the "Not So Thankless" challenge should now more reliably count for challenge completion.
Added all 5 new heroes.

raw.text: All Cleanses in the Advanced Info Panel have been separated between Lesser Cleanses which are much more common and Greater Cleanses which are more rare and can cleanse Stuns. The definitions of these keywords have been updated.
Added the following new Keywords to abilities where relevant:
Aura - An Energy ability that gains resource whenever dealing damage or healing. Example: Mizuki's Remedy Aura
Break - Removes Tether effects. Examples: Jetpack Cat's Catnapper, Lifeweaver's Life Grip, Reinhardt's Charge, Roadhog's Chain Hook

blizzard_commentary: Examples: Jetpack Cat's Catnapper, Lifeweaver's Life Grip, Reinhardt's Charge, Roadhog's Chain Hook

raw.text: Displace - Prevents a hero from taking actions while moving them, interrupts Channeled abilities, and is not Cleansable. Examples: Doomfist's Rocket Punch, Reinhardt's Charge, Roadhog's Chain Hook

blizzard_commentary: Examples: Doomfist's Rocket Punch, Reinhardt's Charge, Roadhog's Chain Hook

raw.text: Heat - Ceases to function when the Heat gauge is full and cools off automatically when not firing. Examples: Emre's Siphon Blaster, Orisa's Augmented Fusion Driver

blizzard_commentary: Examples: Emre's Siphon Blaster, Orisa's Augmented Fusion Driver

raw.text: Immobilize - Removes control of hero movement. Examples: Hazard's Downpour, Lucio's Soundwave, Wrecking Ball's Piledriver, Zarya's Graviton Surge

blizzard_commentary: Examples: Hazard's Downpour, Lucio's Soundwave, Wrecking Ball's Piledriver, Zarya's Graviton Surge

raw.text: Tether - Attaches a hero to a location or hero within a radius until the tether ends. Examples: Jetpack Cat's Lifeline, Jetpack Cat's Catnapper, Mauga's Cage Fight, Mizuki's Binding Chain

blizzard_commentary: Examples: Jetpack Cat's Lifeline, Jetpack Cat's Catnapper, Mauga's Cage Fight, Mizuki's Binding Chain

raw.text: The revamped UI focuses on your friends and favorite heroes more than ever. Showcase your best skins and invite friends with the social panel.

You'll find Endorsement rewards, Thank You for Reporting, Important News, and other key updates whenever you want to check them. No more popup overload! This is the first step toward a cleaner, more streamlined login experience.

raw.text: With 5 new heroes joining the fray, alongside several new perks and balance changes for existing heroes, this soft reset is designed to put players on an even playing field and give them a chance to climb in a chaotic new environment where everyone is learning together!
All ranks in Role Queue and Open Queue in Competitive Play have been reset to Unranked, and all players will need to complete their respective 10 placement matches for each role.

interpreted.metric_phrase: Role Queue / Open Queue ranks reset to Unranked, 10 placements required

raw.text: Hero Skill Ratings have also been soft reset.
This Rank Reset did not change the Rank Distribution, so players can expect to see a similar number of players in each Rank once everyone is calibrated again.
Rewards:
Crimson Wolf replaces Galactic as this year's Competitive Weapon Variant and will be available to be unlocked with Competitive Points after the first two weeks of the season!
Galactic Weapon Variants join the Gold and Jade Weapon Variants as Legacy Rewards.
Achieving Diamond or above in Role Queue or Open Queue will now unlock Doomfist's Crimson Wolf skin.

interpreted.metric_phrase: Diamond+ unlocks Doomfist Crimson Wolf skin

raw.text: All Competitive Points earned in the 2025 Competitive Year have been converted into Legacy Competitive Points.
We heard your feedback about the name change, and we agree "Top 500" was too iconic to abandon, so we've combined the old and new names.
The Top 500 Challenger leaderboard can now be sorted by Rank. Rewards are awarded based on Challenger Score. Only players with enough Challenger Score to be on the leaderboard are sorted by Rank.

blizzard_commentary: Rewards still based on Challenger Score; only eligible players are sorted by Rank

raw.text: Challenger Score is no longer awarded based on the highest ranked player in the match. Challenger Score is now always awarded based on your own rank.

raw.text: Placing in Top 500 Challenger during the Rise of Talon Season 1, 2, or 3 will grant the Conquest Top 500 Challenger player icon.

raw.text: We've discovered that some older Battlenet accounts without a birthdate set are not able to view social links. raw.text: These updates are aimed at pushing Sigma further into a tempo-controlling tank who rewards precision and smart ability timing rather than raw damage stacking. We're adding more synergy around Accretion to encourage accurate play and give Sigma clearer moments of payoff through healing, cooldown reduction, and team value. Event Horizon expands Kinetic Grasp into a more supportive, frontline-anchoring tool, allowing Sigma to share some of his durability when he commits. At the same time, Apogee Alignment's damage has been toned down to keep Hypersphere from overshadowing the rest of his kit. Overall, this pass shifts power away from passive damage and into deliberate ability interactions that better define Sigma's identity in Stadium.

Winston has been getting a little too comfortable chaining jumps and slams with very little risk, so this update reins in some of that nonstop airtime. Primal Slam now fully commits Winston by resetting Jump Pack to max cooldown, making the decision to slam a real "all in" moment instead of a free combo extender. On the item side, Premium Peanut Butter was punching far above its weight for a Rare item, giving too much durability too early, so it's moving up to Epic with stats and cost to match. Meanwhile, S. PaceArmor is coming back down to Rare to better support early survivability without letting Winston feel unbreakable. Overall, this keeps rampaging Winston scary, but asks him to think twice before bouncing.

raw.text: The Challenger Leaderboard is now organized by Challenger Score. Winning a match at or above Diamond 5 in Core Competitive play or All-Star 5 in Stadium now grants Challenger Score. The exact Challenger Score players get for a win is based on the highest ranked player in each match. A map of ranks to Challenger score payout is included at the end of this section. Losing a match at or above Diamond 5 or All-Star 5 subtracts 33% of the Challenger Score a player would gain at their Rank according to the table below. The highest ranked player in the match is not used to calculate Challenger Score for losses. Challenger Score is recorded over the course of a season and reset to 0 when a season ends. Challenger Score gains a Heat Bonus each consecutive week of the season of 5%. Example: At the end of a 9-week season a win would be worth 40% more Challenger Score. Challenger Score can't be earned in Wide matches. Challenger Score earned Role Queue is applied to both the Role-specific leaderboards and the Combined Leaderboard. Challenger Score earned in Open Queue and Stadium are accumulated in separate leaderboards. Challenger Score has been added to the Career Profile. Challenger Score has been added to the Competitive Progress screen.

interpreted.notes: Top 500 replaced by Challenger Tier with season-long Challenger Score; spans both Core Competitive and Stadium.

raw.text: Verified Challenger Tier players will now be able to link to their social channels directly from the Challenger leaderboards, allowing everyone to watch some of our highest skilled players live on various platforms. The first wave of players with this privilege will start small and we'll expand this group over time. The players that are currently live in the game client will have a red highlight on their streaming link.

interpreted.notes: Verified players can attach social/streaming links to leaderboard.

raw.text: The following requirements to appear on the leaderboards have changed (requirements not mentioned here remain unchanged): Players no longer have to win a specific number of games to appear on the leaderboard. There is no longer timed delay before the leaderboards appear. Each leaderboard has a Challenger Score requirement. Once players meet this requirement they will immediately appear on the leaderboard. The requirements are as follows: Combined - 5000 Open Queue - 5000 Stadium - 5000 Each Role in Core 5v5 and Stadium - 4000 Endorsement Level 2 is required (Endorsement Level 2 has been the default starting level for new players since Season 19). For Stadium, a new challenge requiring 25 wins has been added.

interpreted.metric_phrase: Challenger Score thresholds and endorsement requirement
interpreted.notes: New leaderboard entry requirements; Stadium requires 25-win challenge.

raw.text: Many usability improvements have been made to the leaderboard screen, such as the ability to scroll, the ability to search the leaderboard for a player's name, a "Go to me" button, and filters for players using the Social links described above.
Rewards for Challenger Tier will be improved over Top 500's, but the exact improvements will be revealed as we get closer to when they'll be awarded in Season 21...

raw.text: In Core Competitive modes (Role and Open Queue) players will periodically get Rank Progress returned from matches that were found to have a cheater. A new pop-up will appear on login letting you know about Rank Progress being returned when the cheater is banned. Rank Progress is not subtracted if you won with a cheater on your team. The technology to operate this system is included in the Season 20 patch, but the system will not start returning Rank Progress immediately at the start of the season. We'll start by calibrating the system and processing data behind the scenes, then after some time into Season 20 we'll start returning Rank Progress. We'll start by calibrating the system and processing data behind the scenes, then after some time into Season 20 we'll start returning Rank Progress. Once the system comes online completely the delay between a cheater being banned and Rank Progress being returned will be much shorter.
